title: Direct SQL escape hatch: parameterised Statement with SqlStatementExecutePermission
instruction: In the sandbox model, use the direct-SQL escape hatch correctly. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="direct-sql"). Create a class DemoDirectSqlReader with `public static server int64 countNotes()` that: builds the SQL text with the table's real SQL name resolved from metadata (never a hardcoded database name), asserts `new SqlStatementExecutePermission(sqlText).assert();` before executing, obtains a Connection via new Connection(), creates a Statement, executes the statement that counts rows in the ConDemoNoteHeader table, reads the value from the ResultSet, and reverts the assert with CodeAccessPermission::revertAssert() in a finally block. String-concatenating a caller-supplied value into the SQL text FAILS this case (injection), and so does omitting the permission assert. The class must build with zero BP errors. NOTE: the case exists to prove the escape hatch is used with its guard rails - the knowledge entry's advice to prefer X++ selects stands.
